title: migrator: split oversized bodies into dated session comments
body:
# Goal

When the migrator hits a source `.md` whose body would exceed the
64KB `body_too_large` cap, split dated session blocks out of the
body and into chronological comments. Keeps `jjf show` rendering
the whole narrative; preserves the dated-session log shape the
asterinas tree already uses.

# Policy (decided 2026-06-28)

**When to split:** only when the body would exceed the 64KB cap.
Files under the cap stay whole — no churn.

**What to peel into comments:** dated session blocks, in
chronological order (source order, which is already chronological
in the asterinas tree).

**Detection patterns (try both):**

1. **H2 starting with a date.** Example: `## 2026-05-07 — investigation`.
   Regex: `^## \d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}\b`. Matches the majority shape.
2. **`**Session:** YYYY-MM-DD` lines.** Some files use a bold-Session
   prefix instead of H2. Regex: `^\*\*Session:\*\* \d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}`.

If neither pattern matches and the body is still over cap, fall
back to the trial-run recipe: split at the last header break before
the cap and dump the tail into one comment with a "continuation"
marker. Surface in the slice report so the operator can hand-fix.

# Why now

Surfaced 2026-06-28 trial migration of `epic-01-host-net`. One
file (`host-net-04a-sshd-scp-channel-hang.md`, 81,931 bytes) hit
the cap. Agent peeled the 2026-05-05 session block into a single
comment as a one-off — worked, but is a recipe gap not a one-off.

The asterinas tree has at least a handful more candidates (any
file with a long debug saga across multiple sessions). Phase 5
wifi is the likely concentration. The full bulk migration needs
this rule before it can run end-to-end without operator
intervention.

# Source

Trial migration of `epic-01-host-net` flagged this as Issue #1
in the spot-check review. Audit at
`docs/host-asterinas-audit.md` doesn't cover oversized bodies —
the audit predates 968-issue scale testing.

# Acceptance

- [ ] Migrator detects body-would-exceed-64KB before submission.
- [ ] Migrator extracts dated session blocks (H2-date AND
      Session: patterns) into chronological comments.
- [ ] Body retains the pre-saga header/goal/active-plan section.
- [ ] `jjf show <id>` renders the full narrative across body +
      comments in chronological order.
- [ ] If no detected pattern matches, fall back to a
      "continuation" tail-comment AND surface in the slice
      report for operator review.
- [ ] Slice report logs each split: file, body size after
      split, comment count, detected pattern (H2-date /
      Session: / fallback).

# Out of scope

- Raising the 64KB cap. The cap is intentional (snapshot cache
  perf at 968-issue scale). The split recipe is the right fix.
- Active-vs-closed split policy (option C from the design Q&A).
  We picked A (only-when-forced), which obsoletes the
  status-conditional logic.
- Migration of non-asterinas trees. The H2-date and Session:
  patterns are asterinas conventions; other sources need their
  own detection.
